name: UCLA

confRecord.note: 2025 Big Ten Tournament Champions — beat Michigan 5-0. 4th seed. Solans Ormo hat trick.
Big Ten (debut)
Pac-12 Champions
Pac-12
Pac-12
Pac-12

acuAlignNote: Strong — no named kinesiology major but Physiol Sci covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics fully. Motor control/development less explicit.

gpa.note: Highly selective public — aim 3.5+ for strong admission. Athletic scholarship may offset GPA concern.

fin.internationalNote: UCLA out-of-state COA is ~$73k/yr (2025-26). UC regents approved a $3,402 nonresident tuition increase pushing tuition to $52,536. International students not eligible for federal or CA state aid — athletic scholarship is the only lever. A realistic package is 25–50% athletic ($18k–$37k), bringing net cost to ~$36k–$55k. UC public university = less institutional aid flexibility than private schools.

culture.vibe: Vibrant, diverse, high-energy West Coast university culture. UCLA is one of the most socially active campuses in the USA — always something happening. Greek life is prominent but not dominant. International students are common and blend seamlessly.
culture.thingsToDo: Santa Monica Beach (10 min), Venice Beach, Westwood Village restaurants and cinema, Griffith Observatory, LA nightlife, Dodgers/Lakers/Rams games, concerts at the Forum, hiking in the Santa Monica Mountains, Disneyland (45 min). LA is limitless.
culture.socialScene: Large international student body. Australian students have established communities. Bruin athletics culture is intense — games are events. Social media presence huge. Campus feels alive 24/7.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★★ Best campus lifestyle match in guide for Olivier. City, beach, warm, diverse. Feels closest to Sydney energy of any US campus.

facilityDetails.stadium: Wallis Annenberg Stadium — 2,145 capacity, dedicated soccer-only facility opened 2018. FieldTurf, LED videoboard, Musco lighting, grandstand west side. Future phases include permanent locker rooms and training room. Dedicated soccer home since 2018 (previously Drake Stadium).
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 3 dedicated practice fields adjacent to Drake Stadium (2x natural grass, 1x FieldTurf). Ball-wall training walls, rebounder nets, GPS tracking zones marked on all surfaces.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Acosta Athletic Center — 20,000 sq ft dedicated athlete weight room. Olympic lifting platforms (12), squat racks, cable systems, battle ropes, sled tracks. Olympic sports strength coach assigned exclusively to soccer. Cryotherapy chambers, hot/cold plunge pools, normatec recovery boots.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Biomotion Lab (3D motion capture — 24-camera Vicon system), Biomechanics Research Lab (force plates, EMG systems), UCLA Exercise Physiology Lab (VO2 max, lactate testing), GPS/accelerometer tracking (Catapult) on all training sessions. One of the top 3 sports science environments in the NCAA.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UCLA Health dedicated athletic trainer assigned to soccer. On-site physio room, rehabilitation gym, ultrasound, laser therapy, cryotherapy. UCLA Health orthopaedic surgery access (Dr Neal ElAttrache — team physician to LA Lakers).
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Department of Physiological Science: 6 research labs including cardiopulmonary physiology, muscle physiology, biomechanics, and neuroscience. Access to David Geffen School of Medicine anatomy cadaver lab. Student pre-PT club has first-priority clinical placement at UCLA Health.
facilityDetails.extras: Pauley Pavilion athlete lounge and recovery centre. LAFC training facility access (professional relationship). LA Galaxy El Camino College training facility visits. Olympic-standard athlete dining hall. Dedicated locker room for soccer with iPad film stations.
facilityDetails.note: World-class UCLA Athletics S&C and UCLA Health sports medicine available across campus. Dedicated soccer facility with strong institutional support in Los Angeles. Incomplete stadium phases offset by elite university athletic ecosystem.

housing.note: Not strictly mandatory, but incoming freshmen applying by May 1 are guaranteed 4 consecutive years of on-campus housing — one of the strongest housing guarantees in this guide. Verified v38 via official housing.ucla.edu.

coach.name: Ryan Jorden
coach.title: Head Coach (7th season 2025)
coach.profile: 7th season at UCLA 2025. 150-113-35 (.562) overall record. 13 MLS draftees. PhD Biomechanics from Northern Colorado. Won 2025 Big Ten Tournament over Michigan 5-0. USSF A License. Former Pacific Tigers HC — led largest single-season D1 turnaround. NCCAA National CoY 2011 and 2012 at Cal Baptist.

devScoresNote: v42.27 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). §5a Glossary sports-science exemplar — held high. Live uclabruins.com MSOC staff: HC Ryan Jorden (7th yr) + Sergi Nus (Asst) + Kary Whitney (Asst) + Graeme Jorden (GA) = 3 full-time coaches. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Wallis Annenberg Stadium, dedicated soccer-only venue opened 2018 (FieldTurf, LED videoboard, Musco lighting); Acosta Athletic Center with an Olympic-sports strength coach assigned EXCLUSIVELY to soccer (a genuinely dedicated S&C). UCLA's record NCAA-title history is a result and excluded. Tactical trimmed 90->87 to match the 4-coach ACC elite tier (Stanford/ND) — 3 coaches here; technical/fitness held high (88/86) for the dedicated modern stadium and soccer-exclusive S&C, keeping UCLA a clear sports-science exemplar. (Staff: stored Erik Holt -> live Kary Whitney, minor.)
rec: LA lifestyle closest to Sydney. #1 Kinesiology equivalent nationally. Highly competitive for international athletes — elite highlights required. Extraordinary PT/Chiro grad school access via UCLA Health.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 8 of 10 current MFs (80%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 uclabruins.com roster. 2 transferred in — Konstantinos Georgallides (UNC Greensboro then Louisville) and Sean Sent (Washington).
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Yr1/Yr2 flat: 4 rising juniors present, 1 senior clears Yr2. Medium risk means steady not volatile path.

staff.name: Sergi Nus
staff.bg: Full-time assistant. Recruiting and player development.

name: Indiana

confRecord.note: Missed Big Ten Tournament. At-large NCAA bid. Palmer Ault led attack.
Big Ten Champions
Big Ten Champions
Big Ten
Big Ten Champions
Big Ten Champions

proPlayers.draftRank: Top 10 all-time. Most decorated program in US college soccer history.

degreeTitle: B.S. in Kinesiology — Exercise Science Major
acuAlignNote: Near-full alignment — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor control (dedicated lab), motor development, resistance training, sport psych, research, internship. Best motor control coverage of any program in guide.

gpa.note: Indiana is manageable for international athletes with a strong athletic profile. GPA 2.8 borderline.

fin.internationalNote: IU Bloomington out-of-state COA is ~$60k/yr (2025-26). Tuition+fees ~$42k for non-residents. Big Ten athletic aid can be significant. Academic merit available. Bloomington living costs are modest vs coastal cities — aids budget.

culture.vibe: Classic American Big Ten college-town atmosphere at its absolute finest. Bloomington is a college town that punches above its weight — vibrant music scene, excellent restaurants, beautiful forested campus, and intense Hoosiers sports culture.
culture.thingsToDo: Sample Gates and historic campus walks, Lake Monroe hiking and watersports, Brown County State Park (stunning fall foliage), Indianapolis day trips (1hr), IU Auditorium world-class concerts, Little 500 bike race (legendary). Bloomington is surprisingly fun.
culture.socialScene: Warm Midwestern hospitality. International student community exists but the culture is predominantly American. Soccer team is a tight brotherhood — Big Ten culture means athletes are respected on campus. Academic atmosphere is serious but social.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Bloomington is not a city and winters are cold. But the campus energy, athletics culture, and Midwestern warmth make it surprisingly enjoyable. Best if Olivier embraces the quintessential American college experience.

facilityDetails.stadium: Bill Armstrong Stadium — 6,500 capacity, dedicated soccer-only facility, natural grass. Broke single-season attendance record in 2025 (36,735 fans, avg 3,061/match).
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 4 dedicated training fields adjacent to Armstrong Stadium (3 natural grass, 1 synthetic turf). Indoor practice space in Cook Hall for winter months. GPS tracking zones permanently installed on all surfaces.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Simon Skjodt Assembly Hall adjacent Athletic Performance Center — 22,000 sq ft weight facility. Olympic lifting platforms (14), Keiser full system, Hammer Strength equipment. Dedicated strength and conditioning coaches for all Olympic sports. Cryotherapy chambers, normatec recovery, altitude tent room, hyperbaric oxygen chamber.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: IU School of Public Health Kinesiology Labs — 9 dedicated research and teaching laboratories including: bioenergetics and environmental stress suite (unique in the USA — altitude simulation and heat chamber combined), biomechanics lab (12-camera motion capture, 6 force plates), muscle physiology lab (muscle biopsy capability), motor control and coordination lab, cardiovascular physiology lab. GPS tracking (Catapult Vector) with real-time HRV monitoring on all sessions.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Indiana Athletics Sports Medicine — multiple certified athletic trainers. IU Health (University Hospital affiliate) team physician access. Sports concussion research lab on campus. One of the few programs in the guide with a dedicated motor control rehabilitation research lab that doubles as a clinical resource.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: IU Kinesiology: 9 research labs accessible to undergraduates. The bioenergetics and environmental stress lab is the only one of its kind at an American university — nowhere else can simulate altitude AND heat stress simultaneously for research. Students working with faculty on research from Year 1 is the norm, not exception.
facilityDetails.extras: Assembly Hall (17,000 cap basketball) creates extraordinary game-day atmosphere. IU Memorial Union — one of the largest student unions in the USA. IU Outdoor Pool and Wellness Centre. Jacobs School of Music (world-famous) for cultural events. Bloomington itself surprises visitors — voted one of the best college towns in America.
facilityDetails.note: Jerry F. Tardy Center (2020, $7M) — state-of-the-art training, meeting and team room spaces described as unsurpassed in intercollegiate soccer. Top fan atmosphere in college soccer. National championship pedigree.

housing.note: Mandatory for all first-year students; exemptions limited (21+, married/with children, part-time enrollment, transfer with 15+ credits, or special circumstances). Verified v38 via official housing.indiana.edu.

coach.name: Todd Yeagley
coach.title: Head Coach (16th season 2026)
coach.profile: 16th season at Indiana 2026. Son of legendary Jerry Yeagley. 2012 NCAA National Champion (first #16 seed ever to win championship). 39 MLS draftees under him. 11 Big Ten Championships. 4 Big Ten CoY. 4 consecutive Big Ten Tourn titles 2020-23. 2025: missed Big Ten Tourn but received NCAA at-large bid. Former Columbus Crew MLS player. IU All-American 1991-94.

devScoresNote: v42.27 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). ANCHOR — held. Live iuhoosiers.com MSOC staff: HC Todd Yeagley (16th yr) + Christian Lomeli + Tanner Thompson + Jacob Bushue (3 Asst) = 4 full-time coaches. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Bill Armstrong Stadium 6,500 dedicated soccer-only natural grass (single-season attendance record 36,735 in 2025); Athletic Performance Center 22,000 sq ft with dedicated S&C coaches for soccer. Named §5a anchor (most NCAA men's titles); held at 93/88/92.
facilities: School of Public Health Kinesiology Labs
rec: Most decorated soccer program in USA history. Best motor control coverage in this guide (9 labs). Aidan Morris (USMNT) developed here. Bloomington is a classic college town — not a city — and climate is cold. For development and pro pathway if he can compete at Big Ten level.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 5 of 8 current MFs (62%) recruited directly from HS with no prior college, per the live 2025 iuhoosiers.com roster. 3 transferred in — Cristiano Bruletti (Michigan State), Jack Wagoner (Marquette), Jacopo Fedrizzi (Evansville).

staff.name: Christian Lomeli
staff.bg: Full-time assistant. Player development and recruiting.

name: Penn State
acuAlignNote: Strong kinesiology — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor control all covered. Pre-PT advising integrated into kinesiology program.

fin.internationalNote: Penn State out-of-state COA is ~$62k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 35–55% of COA. Merit aid available for international students with strong academic record. State College has low cost of living — package goes further than coastal or urban schools.

gpa.note: Large public research university. Athletic scholarship pathway well-established for internationals.

confRecord.note: 1-6-3 Big Ten (6 pts, 5-8-4 overall). Last in an 11-team Big Ten; did not qualify for the Big Ten Tournament. Verified 2026 via bigten.org official standings.
2-6-2 Big Ten (8 pts, 5-9-2). Last in an 11-team Big Ten.
4-2-2 Big Ten (14 pts, 10-4-5). 2nd in the Big Ten.
3-2-3 Big Ten (12 pts, 6-6-4). 5th in the Big Ten.
6-2-0 Big Ten (18 pts, 13-7-1). Big Ten regular-season champions AND Big Ten Tournament Champions.
6-1-1 Big Ten (10-2-2 overall). 2nd in the regular season; Big Ten Tournament runners-up (lost the final to Indiana) (2020-21 season, played in spring 2021).

devScoresNote: v42.27 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live gopsusports.com MSOC staff: HC Rob Dow + Brad Cole (Assoc HC) + Oscar Umar (Asst) + Owen Jack (Asst) = 4 full-time coaches, plus Adam Gahm (Athletic Performance Coach) and Brandon Hall (Athletic Trainer) listed on the MSOC page — dedicated performance and medical support. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Jeffrey Field Soccer Complex 5,000 dedicated, undergoing a $21.25M renovation (new locker rooms, S&C, hydrotherapy) completing Oct 2025. A well-invested environment — dedicated (renovated) stadium + dedicated performance coach + dedicated ATC — sits above the Creighton ~75 reference. Raised to 78/79/80. (HC change Bob Warming -> Rob Dow is a separate Change Type 2.)

coach.name: Rob Dow
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Rob Dow was named Penn State's head coach in December 2025 after nine seasons at Vermont, where he went 129-41-21, reached five straight NCAA Tournaments and won the 2024 NCAA National Championship. He owns the nation's ninth-best win percentage among active coaches with 5+ seasons and was back-to-back America East Coaching Staff of the Year (2024, 2025). Dow inherits Penn State's 2013 NCAA finalist program and its newly renovated $21.25M Jeffrey Field complex.

facilityDetails.stadium: Jeffrey Field Soccer Complex — 5,000 capacity, natural grass, permanent seating. $21.25M renovation completing Oct 2025: new locker rooms, S&C, hydrotherapy, chairback seating, video boards, indoor bubble.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 dedicated practice fields adjacent to Jeffrey Field. Indoor practice space available in winter.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Penn State Lasch Football Building athletic complex access. Olympic lifting platforms, full weight training suite. Dedicated S&C coach for soccer.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology Department research labs — biomechanics, motor control, exercise physiology. Penn State is a top-20 kinesiology research institution nationally.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Penn State Sports Medicine with certified athletic trainers. Penn State Health access for clinical support.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology Building: biomechanics lab, exercise physiology lab, motor control lab, sport psychology lab. Undergraduate research common.
facilityDetails.extras: Beaver Stadium atmosphere (college football capital). Nittany Lion athletics culture. Strong alumni network across Northeast and Mid-Atlantic.
facilityDetails.note: $21.25M stadium renovation + $31.9M Greenberg Sports Complex (athletic training/wellness, Aug 2025) + $10M indoor practice bubble. Best Big Ten soccer facility investment. Full elite support infrastructure.

housing.note: Mandatory — the Housing and Food Service Contract is built into the offer of admission for all first-year University Park students; exemptions available case-by-case. Verified v38 via official housing.psu.edu / liveon.psu.edu.

culture.vibe: Classic Big Ten college town. State College is a one-university town built around Penn State — everything revolves around Nittany Lions athletics. Extremely social, close-knit community.
culture.thingsToDo: Beaver Stadium (107,000 cap — third largest in the world), downtown State College bars and restaurants, Mount Nittany hiking, Tussey Mountain skiing (20 min), Beaver Lake, Penn State Creamery (legendary ice cream), Philadelphia day trip (3hr).
culture.socialScene: Tight-knit athletics community. Happy Valley has a genuine college-town energy — very American. International student support exists but the culture is predominantly domestic. Soccer team culture is strong brotherhood.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ Rural central Pennsylvania is a significant lifestyle gap from Sydney. No real city, cold winters. Best if Olivier fully embraces the American college-town experience.

staff.name: Rob Dow
staff.bg: Head Coach. Won the 2024 NCAA National Championship at Vermont.

rec: Consistent Big Ten contender. Strong kinesiology program. Rural campus — significant lifestyle trade-off for Olivier.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: All 8 current MFs (100%) recruited directly from HS with no prior college — no transfers on the 2025 roster (last season under prior coach Jeff Cook, before Rob Dow's Dec 2025 hire), per the live gopsusports.com roster.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: ⚠️ 2025 roster only — 2025-26 season unpublished. Classes +1 predicted. 6 of 8 cleared (incl. Liscum Gr, Bell/Grabfelder Jr). Only Siibo and Leavey (Fr→So→Jr) remain as Srs in 2027.

name: Michigan
kinRank: Excellent (Movement Science — nationally recognized program, strong pre-PT pipeline)
acuAlignNote: Excellent — U of M School of Kinesiology is one of the top 3 in the nation. Movement Science covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor control, sport psychology. Michigan Medicine provides elite pre-PT clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: Michigan out-of-state COA is ~$68k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 30–50% of COA. Merit aid limited for international students. Ann Arbor cost of living is moderate. Strong international student community and global alumni network.

gpa.note: Highly selective public university. Athletic scholarship pathway available for international recruits.

confRecord.note: 6-2-2 Big Ten (20 pts, 12-4-4 overall). 3rd seed; Big Ten Tournament runners-up (lost the final to UCLA 0-5). Verified 2026 via bigten.org official standings.
2-3-5 Big Ten (11 pts, 9-5-7). 7th; qualified for the Big Ten Tournament.
2-1-5 Big Ten (11 pts, 5-6-7). 5th in the Big Ten.
1-6-1 Big Ten (4 pts, 4-10-3). Last in a 9-team Big Ten.
4-3-1 Big Ten (8-7-3 overall). 4th in the Big Ten.
5-3-1 Big Ten (6-4-1 overall). 3rd in the Big Ten (2020-21 season, played in spring 2021).

devScoresNote: v42.27 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live mgoblue.com MSOC staff: HC Chaka Daley + Sean Hughes (Assoc HC) + Jhojan Obando (Asst) + Mauro Fuzetti (Asst) = 4 full-time coaches. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Michigan Soccer Stadium 3,000 dedicated natural grass (NCAA host); elite Big Ten S&C complex with a dedicated S&C coach for soccer; School of Kinesiology movement-science labs. Solid environment — 4 coaches + dedicated stadium + Big Ten S&C — but a smaller/older stadium and no dedicated sport scientist verified live. Trimmed from 82/80/83 to 79/78/81 (Duke/Wake tier).

coach.name: Chaka Daley
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Building Michigan into an elite Big Ten program. Former US Youth National Team coach. Reached 2025 Big Ten Tournament Final before 0-5 loss to UCLA. Strong tactical identity — Michigan now Big Ten top-4.

facilityDetails.stadium: Michigan Soccer Stadium — 3,000 capacity, natural grass, dedicated soccer facility. NCAA Tournament host.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 dedicated training fields adjacent to stadium. Michigan Athletic Campus (MAC) indoor facility access for winter.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Michigan Athletics S&C complex — elite Big Ten standard. Olympic lifting, Keiser system, full recovery suite. Dedicated S&C coach for soccer.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: School of Kinesiology Movement Science labs — biomechanics lab, motor behaviour lab, sport performance lab. Michigan Medicine research integration. One of the top 3 kinesiology research environments in the Big Ten.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Michigan Medicine athletic trainers. University of Michigan Health System team physician access. Cutting-edge sports medicine with concussion and return-to-play protocols.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: School of Kinesiology — 8 research labs. Movement Science program has one of the best motor learning and biomechanics lab suites in the Big Ten. Pre-PT pathway supported with Michigan Medicine clinical placements.
facilityDetails.extras: The Big House (107,000 cap) creates campus-wide sports culture. Ann Arbor is one of the most highly-rated college towns in America — genuine food and cultural scene. NWSL and MLS connections through Michigan Alumni network.
facilityDetails.note: Michigan's academic environment is the highlight — Movement Science program and Michigan Medicine access is top-3 in this guide for Olivier's PT/Chiro pathway.

housing.note: Not mandatory, but ~97% of first-years live on campus and housing is guaranteed for the first year if applied for by May 11. Verified v38 via official housing.umich.edu.

culture.vibe: Michigan is a world-class research university with a buzzing Ann Arbor culture. One of the rare Big Ten schools that feels like both a serious academic institution and a social hub. Maize and Blue pride is everywhere.
culture.thingsToDo: Ann Arbor downtown (restaurants, cafes, live music), Detroit day trip (45 min), Great Lakes region access, Michigan athletics games, Nichols Arboretum, East Quad music scene, Ann Arbor Art Fair (largest in USA).
culture.socialScene: Strong international student community (15%+ international). Ann Arbor is voted one of the best college towns in America year after year. Soccer team has a growing culture under Daley — reaching 2025 B1G Final signals program elevation.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Ann Arbor punches well above its Midwest location. The academic environment is exceptional. Climate is cold — four real seasons including harsh winters. Better lifestyle fit than other Midwest options.
culture.lifestyleTags: Ann Arbor culture, academic excellence, cold winters

staff.name: Chaka Daley
staff.bg: Head Coach. Former US Youth National Team coach. Building Michigan to Big Ten top-4 level.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant. Recruiting and player development.

rec: One of the best Movement Science programs in the country. Michigan Medicine pre-PT access is elite. Ann Arbor is a lively college town despite cold winters.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: All 15 current MFs (100%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college — no transfers among midfielders, per the live 2025 mgoblue.com roster.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 9 of 15 MFs cleared, but 5 Fr remain as Jrs creating peer-level competition through Yr1-2. Michigan recruits at Big Ten elite level. Roster count verified by browser MCP; names not captured.

name: Michigan State
acuAlignNote: Solid kinesiology — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics covered. Pre-PT pathway available through kinesiology advising.

fin.internationalNote: Michigan State out-of-state COA is ~$52k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 35–55% of COA. Merit aid available for international students with 3.0+ GPA. East Lansing has low cost of living — package stretches further. Strong kinesiology program.

gpa.note: Accessible large public university. International athlete process well-established.

confRecord.note: 2-5-3 conf record in 11-team Big Ten.
5th in Big Ten standings.
3rd in Big Ten — top-half finish.
7th in Big Ten standings.
2-4-2 conf record in 9-team Big Ten.
COVID-shortened spring season.

devScoresNote: v42.27 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live msuspartans.com MSOC staff: HC Damon Rensing + Reid Friedrichs (Asst) + Nick Barry (Asst) + Zach Carroll (Asst) = 4 full-time coaches, plus Vince Del Valle (Athletic Trainer, Men's Soccer) and Dustin Steider (Assistant Director, Athletic Performance). Stored Tier-1 facilities: DeMartin Soccer Stadium 3,000 dedicated natural grass; standard Big Ten S&C suite; Kinesiology labs. The old 68 reflected conference/results banding; the actual environment (4 coaches + dedicated stadium + dedicated ATC + performance staffer) sits near the Creighton ~75 reference. Corrected up to 72/70/73.

coach.name: Damon Rensing
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Long-tenured MSU coach. Consistent program in competitive Big Ten environment. Developed multiple pro prospects from mid-tier Big Ten position.

facilityDetails.stadium: DeMartin Soccer Stadium — 3,000 capacity, natural grass surface. Dedicated soccer facility on MSU campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 dedicated practice fields adjacent to DeMartin. Indoor facility access in winter months.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: MSU Athletic Performance Center — standard Big Ten S&C suite. Full weight room, Olympic lifting platforms, recovery facilities.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology Department research labs. Exercise physiology, biomechanics labs available for undergraduates. Solid research environment without elite-tier extras.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: MSU Athletic Medicine — certified athletic trainers assigned to soccer. Olin Health Center access for clinical support.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology Building: exercise physiology lab, biomechanics lab, sport psychology lab. Pre-PT pathway available with advising support.
facilityDetails.extras: Spartan Stadium (75,000) creates genuine college atmosphere. East Lansing is a true college town. MSU has one of the best campus settings in the Big Ten.
facilityDetails.note: DeMartin is a functional dedicated facility. Academic labs are solid without being elite. Best suited for mid-tier Big Ten minutes opportunity.

housing.note: Mandatory 2-year (4-semester) live-on requirement, enforced (non-compliance can affect enrollment); exemptions limited (21+ by year end, married, veteran, family address, part-time enrollment). Verified v38 via official liveon.msu.edu.

culture.vibe: Classic Midwest Big Ten college town. East Lansing is a compact, walkable university town with Spartan pride running through everything. Friendly, welcoming Midwestern culture.
culture.thingsToDo: East Lansing downtown restaurants and bars, MSU Horticulture Gardens, Beal Botanical Garden, Lake Lansing (20 min), Detroit day trip (90 min), MSU Planetarium, intramural sports culture.
culture.socialScene: Warm Midwestern welcome. International student community is present but smaller than Michigan or Ohio State. Soccer team is close-knit. Campus is beautiful in fall — brutal in winter.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ Rural feel, cold winters, no nearby city. Better minutes opportunity than elite Big Ten programs. Academic environment is solid. Lifestyle gap from Sydney is significant.

staff.name: Damon Rensing
staff.bg: Head Coach. Long-tenured MSU program builder.

rec: Mid-tier Big Ten. Best for realistic minutes among Michigan schools. Cold campus — similar drawbacks to Michigan.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 8 of 9 current MFs (89%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 msuspartans.com roster — only Miles Merritt transferred in (Incarnate Word).
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 5 So compete as Srs in Yr1 — congested midfield for first season. Opens significantly in Yr2 as 5 seniors exit. Roster count verified; names not captured.

name: Ohio State
kinRank: Good (Exercise Science, large sports medicine research base)
acuAlignNote: Solid exercise science — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics covered. Ohio State Wexner Medical Center provides strong clinical access for pre-PT pathway.

fin.internationalNote: Ohio State out-of-state COA is ~$52k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 35–55% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. Columbus has affordable cost of living — stretches the package further. Large campus with strong international student support.

gpa.note: Large public university. Accessible admission for strong international athletes.

confRecord.note: Defending B1G Tourn champs but failed to qualify in 2025.
2024 Big Ten Tournament Champions.
2023 Big Ten co-regular season champs. Tournament.
Big Ten regular season and tournament titles.
Big Ten conference play.
COVID-shortened spring season.

devScoresNote: v42.27 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live ohiostatebuckeyes.com MSOC staff: HC Brian Maisonneuve + Matt Foldesy (Assoc HC) + Travis Morris (Assoc HC) + Corey Brueggeman (Asst) = 4 full-time coaches, plus a deep dedicated support group on the MSOC page: Patrick Brayfield (Director, Athletic Training), Rebecca Freiburger (Asst S&C), Claire Reinking (Performance Dietitian) and Paul Jones (Associate Sport Scientist). Stored Tier-1 facilities: Jesse Owens Stadium north-end soccer facility 10,000 (shared venue); Woody Hayes flagship S&C. A dedicated sport scientist + dietitian is a real plus; the shared stadium limits the facility score. Raised to 78/76/80.

coach.name: Brian Maisonneuve
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Former MLS and US National Team midfielder. Building Ohio State's soccer identity — 3 consecutive Big Ten titles 2022-24. High-level tactical identity rooted in his professional playing experience.

facilityDetails.stadium: Jesse Owens Memorial Stadium (north end soccer facility) — 10,000 capacity shared venue. Dedicated soccer field with permanent seating.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Ohio State Woody Hayes Athletic Center area practice fields. 2 dedicated soccer training surfaces. Indoor facility access year-round.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Woody Hayes Athletic Center — flagship Ohio State S&C facility. Full Olympic lifting, Keiser system, ice baths, normatec recovery. Shared with football but dedicated Olympic sport windows.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science Department labs — biomechanics, cardiopulmonary physiology, sport performance. Ohio State Wexner Medical Center research integration.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Ohio State Sports Medicine — Wexner Medical Center team physician access. One of the best clinical environments in the Big Ten for pre-PT students. Certified athletic trainers on site.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science undergraduate labs: biomechanics (force plates, motion capture), exercise physiology (metabolic cart, VO2 testing). Pre-PT pathway is well-supported.
facilityDetails.extras: Columbus is a growing, liveable Midwest city — real food scene, Short North Arts District, Columbus Crew MLS team nearby. Ohio Stadium (105,000) — The Horseshoe — creates massive game-day energy.
facilityDetails.note: Ohio State's city lifestyle (Columbus) is its key differentiator in the Midwest. Wexner Medical Center adds genuine PT pathway value. 3 Big Ten titles in 3 years under Maisonneuve signals a peaking program.

housing.note: Mandatory 2-year live-on requirement for full-time, unmarried students within 2 years of high school graduation; exemptions include living within 25 miles or prior post-secondary enrollment. Verified v38 via official housing.osu.edu.

culture.vibe: Ohio State is massive but Columbus genuinely absorbs it — the city and university feel intertwined. Buckeye pride is fanatical. Football dominates fall but soccer has real standing after 3 consecutive Big Ten titles.
culture.thingsToDo: Short North Arts District (restaurant + gallery strip), Columbus Crew MLS (easily walkable), Schottenstein Center concerts, German Village historic neighbourhood, Hocking Hills State Park (1hr), Columbus Zoo.
culture.socialScene: Large diverse campus — 61,000 students. Significant international community. Columbus is a young city with a strong post-grad social scene. Buckeye athletics culture is intense and unifying.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Columbus is the best Midwest city option for lifestyle. Weather still cold but manageable. Soccer tradition has exploded under Maisonneuve. Wexner Medical Center is a genuine PT/Chiro asset.
culture.lifestyleTags: Real city, Buckeye culture, growing Columbus

courses: KINESIO 2550 — Anatomy and Physiology for Health Professions

staff.name: Brian Maisonneuve
staff.bg: Head Coach. Former MLS and USMNT player. 3 consecutive Big Ten titles 2022-24.

rec: Columbus is a real, growing city — good lifestyle for a Midwest option. Wexner Medical Center for pre-PT. Reasonable cost.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: All 8 current MFs (100%) recruited directly from HS with no prior college — no transfers visible anywhere on the roster, per the live 2025 ohiostatebuckeyes.com roster.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 4 of 8 MFs cleared. 3 Fr remain as Jrs + 1 So as Sr in Yr1. Yr3 opens as juniors exit. Roster count verified; names not captured.

name: Northwestern
kinRank: Excellent (Human Sciences — selective interdisciplinary pre-health major)
acuAlignNote: Strong human sciences and kinesiology pathways. Adjacent to Chicago — access to top-tier PT programs and clinical facilities.

fin.internationalNote: Northwestern full COA is ~$82k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship + need-based aid can combine to substantially reduce costs. Northwestern meets demonstrated financial need for athletes. One of the most selective schools in the guide — aid packages reflect the investment.

gpa.note: Highly selective private university. Elite academic profile required alongside athletic ability.

confRecord.note: 3-5-2 Big Ten (11 pts, 8-6-3 overall). 7th; missed the Big Ten Tournament (beat Indiana 2-0 late in the season). Verified 2026 via bigten.org official standings.
3-6-1 Big Ten (10 pts, 9-7-1). 8th in the Big Ten.
4-3-1 Big Ten (13 pts, 10-4-3). 3rd in the Big Ten - a top-half finish.
1-5-2 Big Ten (5 pts, 3-9-5). 8th in a 9-team Big Ten.
3-5-0 Big Ten (6-8-2 overall). 7th in the Big Ten.
2-7-1 Big Ten (2-8-1 overall). 8th in the Big Ten (2020-21 season, played in spring 2021).

devScoresNote: v42.27 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live nusports.com MSOC staff: HC Russell Payne + JR DeRose (Asst/Dir Player Development) + Flo Liu (Asst) + Joe Moulden (Asst) = 4 full-time coaches, plus Jordan Betterman (Director of Analytics) and Tyler Sylvester (Assoc Athletic Trainer). Stored Tier-1 facilities: Martin Stadium 2,500 dedicated natural grass; standard Big Ten S&C; Kinesiology labs. The old 65 was conference/results banding — the actual environment (4 coaches + an analytics director + a dedicated stadium + Big Ten S&C) is materially stronger, comparable to the committed Charlotte +9 correction. Corrected up to 73/72/72. (HC change Tim Lenahan -> Russell Payne is a separate Change Type 2.)

coach.name: Russell Payne
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Russell Payne has led Northwestern since 2021, posting back-to-back winning seasons in 2023-24 and climbing as high as No. 9 in the national rankings. A former USMNT goalkeepers coach (2015-17) and U-23 assistant at the 2024 Paris Olympics, he spent 11 seasons as head coach at Army West Point and was an assistant on Maryland's national-championship staffs. Payne is a top-25 national recruiter (No. 5 class in 2022) with deep professional and international coaching pedigree; Chicago proximity adds strong pre-professional networking for players.

facilityDetails.stadium: Northwestern Soccer Field (Martin Stadium) — 2,500 capacity, natural grass. Lakeside campus setting.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice fields on Evanston campus. Indoor facility access in winter. Chicago transit access makes Northwestern uniquely linked to professional environments.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Northwestern Athletics S&C — standard Big Ten suite. Olympic lifting, recovery facilities. Dedicated S&C for Olympic sports.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Northwestern Kinesiology and Human Sciences labs. Northwestern Feinberg School of Medicine research access. Strong academic environment for sports science study.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Northwestern Medicine — nationally ranked hospital system. Athletic trainers assigned to soccer. Elite pre-PT clinical placement opportunities through Northwestern Medicine network.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Human Sciences: exercise physiology, motor behaviour, human performance labs. Feinberg School of Medicine anatomy resources. One of the best academic environments in this guide for pre-PT/PT research.
facilityDetails.extras: Chicago (20 min by train from Evanston) provides access to MLS Chicago Fire, Rush University Medical Center, Northwestern Memorial Hospital. Ryan Field atmosphere. Lakefront campus on Lake Michigan.
facilityDetails.note: Northwestern's key asset is Chicago access and academic prestige. Feinberg Medicine connection for pre-PT is genuinely elite. Soccer program is competitive, not elite — best for players who prioritise post-soccer career.

housing.note: Mandatory 2-year residency requirement for all incoming first-year undergraduates, guaranteed a space on campus. Verified v38 via official northwestern.edu/living.

culture.vibe: Northwestern is an Ivy-calibre private university with a surprisingly active social scene. Evanston is a beautiful, walkable suburb with genuine cafes, restaurants, and lakefront. Chicago is 20 minutes away by L-train.
culture.thingsToDo: Chicago (L-train, 20 min) — concerts, sports, museums, restaurants. Evanston lakefront, Northwestern Lakefill (stunning), Ryan Field football, Chicago Fire MLS, Navy Pier, Millennium Park, Lincoln Park Zoo.
culture.socialScene: Smaller school (8,000 undergrad) means tighter community. High-achieving student body. International students well-represented. Chicago proximity makes NU unique — social life extends far beyond campus.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Chicago proximity and Northwestern Medicine make this genuinely exciting for Olivier's PT pathway. Soccer is competitive not elite. Very expensive even with aid. Climate is cold — Lake Michigan wind is real.
culture.lifestyleTags: Chicago adjacent, elite academics, lakefront campus

staff.name: Russell Payne
staff.bg: Head Coach. Former USMNT goalkeepers coach; 11 seasons head coach at Army.

staff.name: JR DeRose
staff.bg: Assistant Coach / Director of Player Development.

rec: Elite academics + Chicago access. Soccer is competitive not elite — more realistic minutes than top Big Ten programs. Very expensive.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 9 of 13 current MFs (69%) recruited directly from HS with no prior college, per the live 2025 nusports.com roster. 4 transferred in — Joe Suchecki (Maryland), Yuval Nimrodi (La Salle), Baraka Tarleton (Evansville), Doug Hainer (Coastal Carolina).
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 7 of 13 cleared. No rising seniors (easier Yr1), but 6 Fr remain as Jrs — peer competition is heavy. Roster count verified; names not captured.

name: Wisconsin
kinRank: Good (Exercise and Movement Science, well-established department)
acuAlignNote: Solid exercise and movement science — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, sport psychology covered. UW Health access for clinical placements.

fin.internationalNote: Wisconsin out-of-state COA is ~$50k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 35–55% of COA. Merit aid available for international students with 3.0+ GPA. Madison has moderate cost of living. Large campus with strong international student support and kinesiology program.

gpa.note: Accessible large public university. Strong athletic department support.

confRecord.note: 6th in Big Ten standings.
10th in Big Ten standings.
6th in Big Ten standings.
6th in Big Ten standings.
3-4-1 conf record in 9-team Big Ten.
COVID-shortened spring season.

devScoresNote: v42.27 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live uwbadgers.com MSOC staff: HC Neil Jones + Nate Boyden (Asst) + Simon Brown (Asst/Recruiting) + Tyler Smith (Asst) = 4 full-time coaches, plus George Ikemizu (Assistant S&C, shared M-Soccer/Track) and Chris Peroutka (Athletic Trainer, Men's Soccer). Stored Tier-1 facilities: McClimon Soccer Complex 3,000 dedicated artificial turf; McClain Center shared flagship S&C; Kinesiology labs. Old 66 was conference/results banding; the actual environment (4 coaches + dedicated turf stadium + Big Ten S&C + dedicated ATC) sits near the Creighton reference. Corrected up to 72/70/72. (HC change John Trask -> Neil Jones is a separate Change Type 2.)

coach.name: Neil Jones
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Neil Jones has led Wisconsin since 2022, improving the Badgers to an eight-win 2025 season. An Auckland, New Zealand native and former New Zealand youth international, he spent nine seasons as head coach at Loyola Chicago (82-54-26, two Missouri Valley titles, NCAA Tournament appearances) and was part of UC Santa Barbara's 2006 NCAA Championship staff and Northwestern's Big Ten title runs as associate head coach. USSF-licensed with USA national-team scouting experience.

facilityDetails.stadium: McClimon Soccer Complex — 3,000 capacity, artificial turf surface. Dedicated soccer facility on UW campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 dedicated training surfaces at McClimon. Indoor facility access in winter.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: McClain Center — UW Athletics flagship training facility. Full S&C suite, Olympic lifting, recovery facilities. Shared with all Olympic sports.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Department of Kinesiology labs. Exercise physiology, biomechanics, motor control labs available. UW-Madison is a top-15 research university — academic quality is strong.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UW Health Sports Medicine — team physicians and certified athletic trainers. One of the more accessible PT pathway environments in the Big Ten.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology Building: exercise physiology lab, biomechanics lab, human performance lab. Solid Big Ten standard. UW Health integration for clinical placements.
facilityDetails.extras: Madison is one of the most liveable college cities in the Midwest — Lake Mendota and Lake Monona are on campus. State Capitol is walkable. Madison tech startup scene creates post-graduation pathways.
facilityDetails.note: Wisconsin's best asset is Madison itself — a highly liveable city on the lakes. Soccer is mid-tier Big Ten. Academic environment is strong. Cold winters are the main drawback.

housing.note: Not mandatory, but 90%+ of freshmen live on campus and a space is guaranteed for those who complete the housing contract and deposit by May 1. Verified v38 via official housing.wisc.edu.

culture.vibe: Madison is unique — a capital city built around a university, on an isthmus between two lakes. Far more liveable than typical Midwest college towns. Badgers culture is intense especially for football and basketball.
culture.thingsToDo: Lake Mendota and Monona (kayaking, sailing, winter ice skating), State Street (bars, restaurants, boutiques), Wisconsin State Capitol, Henry Vilas Zoo, Madison Symphony, Madison Mallards baseball.
culture.socialScene: Large diverse campus. International students present and supported. Madison itself is young and social — beyond the university, the city has its own identity. Soccer team culture is friendly and mid-intensity.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Madison is genuinely liveable — far better lifestyle than Illinois or MSU. Lakes on campus are beautiful in fall. Winters are brutal. Best minutes opportunity among Midwest Big Ten programs.
culture.lifestyleTags: Lakes city, liveable Madison, cold winters

staff.name: Neil Jones
staff.bg: Head Coach. Prior nine seasons head coach at Loyola Chicago.

rec: Lower-cost Big Ten option. Best for minutes opportunity among mid-tier programs. Cold Wisconsin winters are a significant drawback.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 3 of 7 current MFs (43%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 uwbadgers.com roster; 4 transferred in — Robert Kaemmerer (Oregon State), Alejandro Coury (Colgate), Ola Arntsen (Louisville), Gianluca Del Priore (Niagara).
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 3 of 6 MFs cleared. Balanced competition — 2 So as Srs in Yr1, 1 Fr as Jr. Wisconsin rebuilds modestly year-to-year. Roster count verified; names not captured.

name: Rutgers
acuAlignNote: Solid exercise science program. NYC proximity means excellent clinical placement opportunities for pre-PT pathway.

fin.internationalNote: Rutgers out-of-state COA is ~$62k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 35–55% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. NJ location with proximity to NYC — moderate-to-high living costs. Public flagship with large international student population.

gpa.note: Accessible large public university. Athletic scholarship packages standard for internationals.

confRecord.note: 3-5-2 Big Ten (11 pts, 8-6-3 overall). 8th in the Big Ten; earned an NCAA Tournament at-large bid (RPI ~38). Verified 2026 via bigten.org official standings.
3-6-1 Big Ten (10 pts, 6-9-2). 9th in the Big Ten.
3-4-1 Big Ten (10 pts, 5-8-3). 7th in the Big Ten.
4-2-2 Big Ten (14 pts, 10-5-6). 3rd in the Big Ten.
3-4-1 Big Ten (9-6-2 overall). 5th in the Big Ten.
4-4-0 Big Ten (4-4-1 overall). 5th in the Big Ten (2020-21 season, played in spring 2021).

devScoresNote: v42.27 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live scarletknights.com MSOC staff: HC Jim McElderry + Gavin Wyse (Assoc HC) + Tyler Stakiwicz (Assoc HC) + Joe Mortarulo (Asst) = 4 full-time coaches, plus a Director of Operations. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Yurcak Field 5,000 dedicated artificial turf (larger than the NW/Wisc/MSU venues); RWJ Barnabas Health Athletic Performance Center (strong dedicated Big Ten S&C). Old 68 was conference/results banding; the larger dedicated stadium + strong performance center place it slightly above the NW/Wisc/MSU cluster. Corrected up to 73/72/74.

coach.name: Jim McElderry
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Long-tenured Rutgers coach. NYC proximity creates unique professional networking for soccer players. 2025 RPI No.38 shows program above its Big Ten standing.

facilityDetails.stadium: Yurcak Field — 5,000 capacity, artificial turf. Dedicated Rutgers Soccer facility on Busch Campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice fields on Busch Campus. Indoor facility access in winter. NYC transit (NJ Transit, 40 min to Manhattan) makes Rutgers uniquely accessible to professional environments.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: RWJ Barnabas Health Athletic Performance Center — strong Big Ten S&C suite. Olympic lifting, full recovery facilities. Dedicated strength coaches for Olympic sports.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science Department labs — biomechanics, exercise physiology, sport performance. NYC proximity means access to NYU Langone, Hospital for Special Surgery research networks.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Rutgers RWJ Medical — affiliated university hospital. Athletic trainers assigned to soccer. One of the better clinical access environments in the Big Ten for pre-PT.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science: physiology lab, biomechanics lab, human performance labs. RWJ Barnabas Health affiliation enables pre-PT clinical placements in the NYC metro area.
facilityDetails.extras: New York City 40 min by train — genuine access to MLS NY Red Bulls (Rutgers-adjacent), NYCFC, professional sports, medical centres. New Brunswick is a diverse college city with growing restaurant and cultural scene.
facilityDetails.note: Rutgers' hidden strength is NYC access — no other Big Ten program offers this proximity to professional soccer, medicine, and career networking. Exercise Science program solid without being elite.

housing.note: Not an explicit blanket requirement, but first-years must actively participate in the housing selection process to secure a space — those who opt out are not guaranteed housing. Verified v38 via official ruoncampus.rutgers.edu.

culture.vibe: Rutgers is a large public university in a diverse NJ city with one of the most unique geographic advantages in college soccer — New York City 40 minutes away. Campus itself is spread across New Brunswick with a genuine multicultural feel.
culture.thingsToDo: NYC day trips (Red Bulls, NYCFC, MLS), New Brunswick restaurant scene (excellent for a college city), Princeton (30 min), Jersey Shore (1hr), Rutgers football culture, NJ Performing Arts Center (Newark, 30 min).
culture.socialScene: Very diverse student body — large international and South Asian community in particular. New Brunswick has genuine social infrastructure beyond the university. NYC proximity means weekends can be Manhattan.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ NYC proximity is the differentiator — closest to a real international city for any Midwest/Northeast Big Ten option. Exercise science is solid. Soccer is mid-tier. Cold winters, but less brutal than Midwest.
culture.lifestyleTags: NYC adjacent, diverse campus, genuine city access

staff.name: Jim McElderry
staff.bg: Head Coach. Long-tenured Rutgers coach. NYC network builder.

rec: NYC proximity is a genuine advantage. Mid-tier Big Ten program. Historically strong in Big East era — still rebuilding in Big Ten.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 10 of 11 current MFs (91%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 scarletknights.com roster — only Francesco Di Ponzio transferred in (Indian Hills CC, JUCO).
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 5 of 8 MFs cleared before 2027. 2 So compete as Srs in Yr1. Solid Big Ten opportunity with manageable competition. Roster count verified; names not captured.

name: Washington
kinRank: Good (Kinesiology, strong sports medicine research ties)
acuAlignNote: Solid kinesiology — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics covered. UW Medicine provides strong clinical access for pre-PT pathway.

fin.internationalNote: Washington out-of-state COA is ~$52k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 35–55% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. Seattle living costs are high and rising — factor into net cost. Strong international student community and research profile.

gpa.note: Selective public university. International athletes well-supported through athletic department.

confRecord.note: 7-3-0 Big Ten (21 pts, 16-6-2 overall). 2nd in the Big Ten in its second season after moving from the Pac-12. Verified 2026 via bigten.org official standings.
4-3-3 Big Ten (15 pts, 8-6-7). 4th in the Big Ten - Washington's first Big Ten season after leaving the Pac-12.
2-4-4 Pac-12 (5-6-7 overall). 5th in the Pac-12.
7-1-2 Pac-12 (15-2-3 overall). Pac-12 regular-season champions.
6-1-2 Pac-12 (18-2-2 overall). 2nd in the Pac-12.
7-3-0 Pac-12 (12-4-0 overall). 2nd in the Pac-12 (2020-21 season, played in spring 2021).

devScoresNote: v42.27 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live gohuskies.com MSOC staff: HC Jamie Clark + Mark Collings (Assoc HC) + Richard Reece (Asst) + Ryan Fahey (Asst) = 4 full-time coaches, plus a Director of Operations and a shared Assistant Athletic Trainer. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Husky Soccer Stadium 2,200 dedicated, brand-new natural-grass surface installed summer 2025; Dempsey Indoor; dedicated strength coach for soccer. A strong head coach (Clark, multiple College Cup appearances) plus a brand-new dedicated stadium place it slightly above the NW/Wisc/MSU cluster. Corrected up to 74/73/74.

coach.name: Jamie Clark
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: 2025 NCAA National Champions under Jamie Clark — the program's first men's soccer national title, following a 2021 NCAA final run and four Pac-12 Coach of the Year awards. Seattle Sounders relationship a genuine pro pathway asset (30+ MLS players produced). Strong international recruitment track record.

facilityDetails.stadium: Husky Soccer Stadium — 2,200 capacity, dedicated soccer facility. Brand new natural grass surface installed summer 2025. Selected as 2026 FIFA World Cup practice site.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 practice fields adjacent to Husky Soccer Stadium. Husky Stadium (73,000) creates massive college athletics culture context.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Dempsey Indoor — UW Olympic Sports training facility. Full S&C suite, Olympic lifting, recovery facilities. Dedicated strength coach for soccer.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: UW Kinesiology — exercise physiology, biomechanics, motor control labs. UW Medicine research integration. Strong research environment in exercise science.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UW Medicine — top-5 nationally ranked medical system. Athletic trainers and team physicians. Pre-PT clinical placement through UW Medicine network is genuine elite access.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology Building: exercise physiology lab, biomechanics lab, motor control lab. UW Medicine anatomy and physiology resources. Pre-PT pathway well-supported with clinical placements.
facilityDetails.extras: Seattle Sounders MLS (Jamie Clark has genuine relationship) — pro pathway asset. Pike Place Market, Space Needle, waterfront. Seattle is a major tech and healthcare city. Amazon, Microsoft campuses nearby.
facilityDetails.note: UW Medicine Sports Medicine Center on campus — ultramodern 30,000sqft facility. World Cup 2026 selection validates facility quality. Used historically by Celtic, Barcelona for pre-season. Strong UW athletic support ecosystem.

housing.note: Not mandatory (unlike some peer schools, UW Seattle has no first-year live-on requirement), but over 70% of first-years choose on-campus housing. Verified v38 via official hfs.uw.edu.

culture.vibe: University of Washington sits on the shores of Lake Washington in one of America's most beautiful cities. Seattle has world-class food, tech industry energy, outdoor adventure, and Sounders soccer culture. Campus is stunning.
culture.thingsToDo: Seattle Sounders MLS (Stadium 10 min), Pike Place Market, Space Needle, Capitol Hill restaurants and nightlife, Lake Washington kayaking, Cascade mountain hiking and skiing (90 min), San Juan Islands, Vancouver BC day trip (3hr).
culture.socialScene: Highly international campus — strong tech and Pacific Rim student communities. Seattle's international culture (Japanese, Korean, Filipino, Chinese communities all strong) makes it genuinely diverse. Soccer has a real fan base.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Seattle is genuinely great — real international city with tech energy and outdoor culture. UW Medicine is elite for PT/Chiro pathway. Rain is heavy and constant — climate is the one real drawback. Sounders access is a pro bonus.
culture.lifestyleTags: Seattle city, Sounders access, rainy Pacific Northwest

staff.name: Jamie Clark
staff.bg: Head Coach. Building Washington through Big Ten transition. Seattle Sounders relationship a genuine pro pathway asset.

rec: Seattle city lifestyle. Sounders proximity a pro soccer bonus. Rain is a genuine drawback for Olivier. Mid-range cost.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: All 14 current MFs (100%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college — zero transfers anywhere on the entire 2025 roster, per the live gohuskies.com roster. Notable given Washington won the 2025 NCAA national title (Jamie Clark's first) built entirely through direct recruiting, not the transfer portal.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: ⚠️ 2025 roster only — 2025-26 unpublished. Classes +1 predicted. 8 of 13 cleared, but 5 Fr (→ Srs by 2027) create heavy competition in Yr1. Opens well in Yr2 as seniors exit.

name: Maryland

confRecord.note: Big Ten Regular Season Champions. Cirovski 32nd season. Led nation in attendance.
3rd in Big Ten — NCAA appearance. Cirovski maintaining top-25 program.
9th in Big Ten standings — NCAA Tournament appearance.
Big Ten Regular Season Champions — NCAA appearance.
NCAA Tournament. Consistent top-25 program under Cirovski.
COVID-shortened season.

titles: Most MLS World Cup players of any program (6 FIFA World Cup appearances)

proPlayers.draftRank: Elite all-time — 49 MLS picks + 7 homegrown under Cirovski
notable: Graham Zusi — USMNT (2014 World Cup), Sporting KC legend
Robbie Rogers — USMNT, LA Galaxy MLS Cup 2014
Joshua Bolma — 2023 R1 #4 New England Revolution

acRank: Strong — B.S. Kinesiology with comprehensive exercise science
kinRank: B.S. Kinesiology — top-20 kinesiology program nationally, dedicated exercise science labs
acuAlignNote: Strong alignment — Maryland's Kinesiology program is top-20 nationally and covers the vast majority of ACU BESS units including anatomy, biomechanics, exercise physiology, motor control, resistance training, sport psychology and research methods. Strong pre-PT advising track. Baltimore/DC hospital network provides exceptional clinical observation hours. Missing a few ACU-specific advanced units.
soccerLevel: Big Ten — D1 Elite. 3× National Champions. Cirovski era dominant.
prePT: Excellent — top kinesiology program + Baltimore/DC hospital clinical network

gpa.note: Maryland is selective but accessible (45% acceptance). GPA 2.8 is borderline — athletic scholarship is the key lever. Kinesiology is a rigorous major requiring maintained GPA.

fin.internationalNote: Out-of-state tuition ~$38k. Athletic scholarship can cover majority. Merit aid available. Maryland has strong international student support in athletics.

culture.vibe: Large public research university adjacent to Washington D.C. — the best of both worlds. College Park is a classic college town but D.C. Metro access means the capital is 30 minutes away. Massive campus (40,000 students) with a vibrant, diverse community. Soccer culture strong under Cirovski.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ College Park is not warm (cold winters) but D.C. access provides city lifestyle. Large university with great diversity and international community. Soccer program is elite with the best all-time professional pipeline in the guide. D.C. and Baltimore clinical access for PT is exceptional.
culture.socialScene: Huge university social scene. Diverse, international campus. D.C. and Baltimore easily accessible. Maryland basketball and football rival athletics culture. Soccer draws strong local support — led nation in attendance 2019 and 2025.
culture.thingsToDo: Washington D.C. (30min Metro) — all major attractions, DC United (MLS), national monuments. Baltimore (45min) — Inner Harbor, Orioles baseball, Ravens football. Chesapeake Bay beaches (2hr). Philadelphia (2.5hr). Ocean City MD beaches.
culture.lifestyleTags: Near D.C., large university, cold winters, elite soccer culture

facilityDetails.stadium: Ludwig Field — 5,000 capacity. Natural grass. Historic venue — Maryland led nation in attendance 2016, 2019, 2025. On-campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Extensive training facilities adjacent to Ludwig Field. Maryland winters limit outdoor training Oct-Mar.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Xfinity Center S&C facilities — world-class. Maryland Athletics invests heavily across all sports. GPS tracking, Olympic platforms, Hammer Strength.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Full sports science setup — GPS (Catapult), video analysis (Hudl + XOS), lactate testing, VO2max. Cirovski is a pioneer in sports science integration in college soccer.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Maryland's School of Public Health (Kinesiology dept) — one of the top-20 kinesiology programs nationally. Biomechanics labs, exercise physiology suites, motor control labs, strength & conditioning research facilities. Research opportunities with faculty on exercise science topics.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Maryland Athletics Sports Medicine — full-time staff, team physician. University of Maryland Medical System (UMMS) proximity — one of the Mid-Atlantic's leading academic medical centres. Clinical observation hours accessible through UMMS partnership.
facilityDetails.extras: Ludwig Field atmosphere is unique — home crowd passion unmatched in college soccer. D.C. Metro access. Baltimore access. Chesapeake Bay area. Large, diverse campus community.
facilityDetails.note: Maryland offers elite-level soccer development (49 MLS picks under Cirovski), a top-20 kinesiology program, and world-class clinical access through the Baltimore/DC hospital network. Cold winters and large-campus environment are the main trade-offs.

housing.note: Not mandatory, but 9 of 10 UMD freshmen live on campus and priority is given to those meeting the May 1 enrollment/housing deadline. Verified v38 via official reslife.umd.edu.

coach.name: Sasho Cirovski
coach.title: Head Men's Soccer Coach
coach.profile: Sasho Cirovski — 32nd season at Maryland. 3× NCAA National Champion (2005, 2008, 2018). 49 MLS SuperDraft picks + 7 MLS homegrown signings. 6 players appeared at FIFA Men's World Cup. Produced Zack Steffen, Graham Zusi, Omar Gonzalez, Taylor Twellman. Led nation in attendance 2016, 2019, and 2025. One of the greatest coaches in college soccer history. 2018 National Coach of the Year.

devScoresNote: v42.27 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). ANCHOR — held. Live umterps.com MSOC staff: HC Sasho Cirovski + Brian Rowland (Assoc HC) + Steve Armas (Assoc HC) + Michael Gould (Asst) = 4 full-time coaches, plus a genuinely dedicated support ecosystem listed on the MSOC page: Ben Hoffman (Strength & Conditioning), Katelyn Engen (Athletic Trainer), Paula Karamihas (Sports Nutrition), a mental-health counselor and a student-athlete-development lead. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Ludwig Field 5,000, world-class Xfinity S&C, full sports science — GPS (Catapult) + video (Hudl + XO). Named §5a anchor; held at 93/91/90.
rec: Maryland is the most decorated professional pipeline program in the entire guide — 49 MLS picks and 6 World Cup players under Cirovski. The B.S. Kinesiology is top-20 nationally with excellent pre-PT preparation and world-class clinical access through the Baltimore/DC hospital network. College Park is adjacent to D.C. providing city lifestyle. Cold winters and lack of warm climate are the main negatives for Olivier. Elite choice if climate is not the priority.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 7 of 9 current MFs (78%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 umterps.com roster. 2 transferred in — Joseph Umberto Picotto (UMBC) and Chris Steinleitner (Eastern Florida State College, JUCO).
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Maryland has consistently produced MLS first-round picks. Squad depth is elite. Cirovski recruits nationally and internationally. Olivier would need 1-2 development years before meaningful minutes are realistic.
